questions.question: What is the size of the 'int' data type in most modern 64-bit C++ compilers?
questions.explanation: In most 64-bit C++ compilers, 'int' occupies 4 bytes.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents a hexadecimal number in C++?
questions.explanation: C++ uses the prefix '0x' or '0X' to represent hexadecimal values.

questions.question: What is the size of the 'double' data type in C++?
questions.explanation: A 'double' is typically 8 bytes (64-bit floating-point) in modern C++.

questions.question: What is the output of the following expression in C++? 15 / 4
questions.explanation: Integer division in C++ truncates decimals, so 15 / 4 = 3.

questions.question: Which data type should be used in C++ to store a large integer value (greater than 2 billion)?
questions.explanation: 'long long' provides at least 64-bit storage, handling large integer values beyond int limits.
